mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate compresses equity discount rates and lifts valuation multiples, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS levels below 0.25% are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income expectations and forced de-risking by institutional portfolios, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE has risen >0.5pp in the prior 3 months during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and retail sales indicates a broad demand-supply collapse that amplifies recession probabilities beyond either signal alone, generating strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of INDPRO and RSAFS does not produce a Rank IC magnitude above 0.04 against 20-day S&P 500 returns in any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve holds rates near the zero lower bound, month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation reduce real discount rates and boost equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases do not coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 52% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly increases in housing starts reflect strengthening consumer confidence and credit availability, which precede positive wealth effects and equity inflows, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive monthly increases in HOUST are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of triggered instances during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls signals aggregate wage income expansion, supporting consumption forecasts and top-line revenue estimates; positive changes predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS increases do not lead to positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains static near zero, real policy rates fall and stimulate risk appetite; this interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of accelerating CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction term exhibits a t-statistic below 1.5 in rolling 3-year regressions.
